When a stock belongs to an established same-sector sell-the-news cluster (≥3 peers within ~8 weeks post-earnings at -5 to -8% on comparable beat-with-soft-guide setups), anchor the magnitude range on the peer median/lower-quartile rather than the dollar size of the headline guide delta.

A 'headline beat + slight guide miss' is a trap when segment composition shows a long-running growth segment inflecting negative YoY for the first time — weight segment mix changes and prior-trend-breaks heavier than the aggregate guide delta when sizing downside.

Extend the expected downside range when the stock has rallied ≥8% in the 2 weeks into print AND a peer sell-the-news precedent exists — extended positioning compresses the upside and expands the tail on any blemish, so confidence in the direction should translate into a wider, lower range (not just a wider range).

In the 2023+ hyperscaler-AI-capex regime, semiconductor prints are graded on the composition of forward revenue, not the headline guide delta. When AI-exposed lines are guided strong but ex-AI semis (broadband/storage/wireless/industrial) are flat-to-negative YoY, the AI narrative is already priced and the marginal information is the ex-AI deterioration — expect multiple compression on names where AI does not yet dominate the mix.

When a single-stock drawdown of ≥5% occurs on a flat-to-positive sector tape with zero sell-side downgrades and broadly raised price targets (as on AVGO Sept 1, 2023), the move is positioning-driven (crowded-long unwind / profit-taking on a pre-print run-up), not a fundamental re-rating — slow or bullish sell-side reaction is NOT a leading indicator of next-session mean-reversion support and should not be used to narrow downside ranges.

These four large-cap semis formed a summer-2023 sell-the-news cluster (all -5 to -8% day-of-print on beats with incrementally soft guides). When predicting any of the four going forward, explicitly consult the other three's most recent prints in the same earnings cycle as a magnitude anchor — the cluster grades each name against the same AI-vs-ex-AI composition template.
